title: Tium leibergii
taxonomy: Tium leibergii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. It is a rare plant endemic to Idaho in the United States. The plant is known by several common names, including Leiberg's buttercup, mountain buttercup, and Leiberg's crowfoot. This flowering plant is often found in moist areas with rich soil, such as meadows and along streams. Tium leibergii is considered a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
